references.text:
[Er63c] Erd\H{o}s, P., On a problem in graph theory. Math. Gaz. (1963), 220--223.
 
 [SzSz65] Szekeres, E. and Szekeres, G., On a problem of Sch\"{u}tte and {E}rd\H{o}s. Math. Gaz. (1965), 290--293.

remarks.text:
Sch\"{u}tte asked Erd\H{o}s this in the early 1960s.

It is easy to check that $f(1)=3$ and $f(2)=7$. Erd\H{o}s \cite{Er63c} proved\[2^{n+1}-1 \leq f(n) \ll n^22^n.\]Szekeres and Szekeres \cite{SzSz65} proved that $f(3)=19$ and\[n2^n \ll f(n).\]

statement.text: Let $f(n)$ be minimal such that there is a tournament (a complete directed graph) on $f(n)$ vertices such that every set of $n$ vertices is dominated by at least one other vertex. Estimate $f(n)$.
